implementation_summary:
Announce-on-change (profile.tsx): broadcastProfile (called from handleNicknameBlur/handleAvatarSelect, unchanged) now ADDITIONALLY calls a new fanOutProfileAnnounceOnChange, alongside the existing unmodified `void publishProfileUpdate(next)` line. Extracted two exported, dependency-injectable pure/testable functions (mirrors ProfileHealWatcher.tsx's planDueSweep/advanceAfterFire split, since this repo has no jsdom/renderHook precedent): planProfileAnnounceFanout(contacts) — PURE, filters to active/non-archived, case-folds pubkeyHex, assigns a stagger delay (0 when audience <= PROFILE_EDIT_FANOUT_STAGGER_THRESHOLD=5, else spread across PROFILE_EDIT_FANOUT_STAGGER_WINDOW_MS=30s, same values as the S05 watcher's own constants, kept independent per architecture.md's boundary rules); and executeProfileAnnounceFanout(params) — synchronous, fire-and-forget, fires (or hands to an injectable scheduleDelay) one send.ts#sendProfileAnnounce call per plan entry, swallowing per-target failures. The React wiring reads listContacts(ownPubkeyHex, {includeArchived:true}) LIVE at call time, lazily resolves an NDK instance via connectNdk (dynamic import, matching this file's own pre-existing lazy-import pattern), and never blocks/awaits the fan-out itself.

Announce-on-pair (pairingAck.ts): sendPairingAck (scanner side) fires one sendProfileAnnounce to issuerPubkeyHex, using its own already-explicit ndk/ownPubkeyHex/ownPrivateKeyHex params plus storage.ts#readUserProfile() for the current local profile, immediately after the pairing-ack itself successfully publishes; any announce failure is caught in its own try/catch and never turns a successful ack into 'queued-for-retry'. No signature change. handlePairingAck (issuer side) gained a new OPTIONAL `opts.ndk` field (backward compatible with every existing/omitted caller): on a FRESH 'admitted' outcome only (never on 'already-admitted'), when opts.ndk is supplied, it derives its own pubkeyHex via nostrKeys.ts#derivePublicKeyHex(ownPrivateKeyHex) (already received, so no new required param) and fires sendProfileAnnounce to the freshly-admitted sender using storage.ts#readUserProfile(). This is landed as 'Step 10' in the admission block, directly after rememberKnownPeers/rememberContact/pairingAckAdmissions.set and BEFORE where story 07's importCard(senderHex, decoded.profile) must land next (an explicit code comment marks the insertion point for S07 — see notes below).

Both admission points reuse send.ts#sendProfileAnnounce verbatim — no wrap/publish re-implementation, no new kind, no gate-logic change; receive.ts is untouched (a pushed announce is handled by the existing announce arm unmodified).

SCOPE AMENDMENT (lead-authorized): the initial pass flagged that handlePairingAck's sole real caller, welcomeSubscription.ts#subscribeToWelcomes, already had `ndk` in scope at its handlePairingAck(...) call site but did not pass it, leaving the issuer-side announce fully implemented/unit-tested but INERT in the running app. The lead reviewed the finding and authorized adding welcomeSubscription.ts to this story's scope for a minimal, additive change: the existing handlePairingAck(giftWrapEvent, ownPrivateKeyHex) call now passes a third argument, `{ ndk }` — the function's own pre-existing `ndk` param, already in closure scope at that line. No other line in welcomeSubscription.ts changed: the kind-1059 subscription filter, dispatch order, and Welcome/join-request fallthrough are byte-for-byte unchanged (verified by a new source-scan regression test). A second new test (call-through spy on the real, unmocked handlePairingAck) proves the call site now passes exactly `{ ndk }`. The issuer-side push-trigger announce is now live end-to-end — AC-PROF-11b is fully satisfied on both the scanner and issuer sides, not merely unit-tested in isolation.

escalation_history.reason: Issuer-side push-trigger announce (AC-PROF-11b) required handlePairingAck to receive an ndk instance, but its sole real caller (welcomeSubscription.ts) was outside this story's declared files list — flagged rather than silently touched, per the story's explicit scope restriction.
escalation_history.decider_caveats: Lead authorized a minimal, additive scope amendment: add welcomeSubscription.ts for the single handlePairingAck(...) call-site argument only (pass its own already-in-scope `ndk` through as opts.ndk). No change to the subscription filter, dispatch order, or Welcome/join-request fallthrough. Implemented and verified with a source-scan regression test plus a call-through spy; full suite green, typecheck clean.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: handlePairingAck's own architecture (receive-path modules never hold a singleton NDK reference, per architecture.md) is structurally incompatible with firing an outbound announce from inside it, and the story's originally-declared files list (exactly profile.tsx + pairingAck.ts) did not include handlePairingAck's real caller (welcomeSubscription.ts), which is where the needed `ndk` value already lives. Reconciling 'implement the AC's issuer-side push exactly where the story says it must live' against 'never touch a file outside declared scope' required first landing an OPTIONAL opts.ndk field (backward compatible, but inert without a caller passing it), flagging the gap explicitly rather than silently fixing or silently skipping it, and then — once the lead reviewed the finding and authorized the scope amendment — making the single call-site edit that actually wires it live. A pre-existing test in pairingAck.test.ts ('handlePairingAck cannot publish anything at all') also encoded a now-partially-obsolete structural claim that had to be re-scoped to the omitted-opts.ndk case specifically, rather than an absolute claim, without breaking its original intent (AC-PRIV-1: no unaddressed kind-0).
retrospective.surprised_by: sendPairingAck's push-trigger announce needed a source for the CURRENT local profile (nickname+avatar), but SendPairingAckParams.ownProfile only carries {nickname, createdAt} (no avatar) and its callers (pendingIntent.ts, add.tsx) are outside this story's scope, so widening that param shape was not an option. storage.ts#readUserProfile() (a plain localStorage read, already used by ProfileContext.tsx) turned out to be exactly the right zero-new-param seam for both admission points, and is safely no-op-default (empty nickname) in a non-browser/no-jsdom test environment rather than throwing — this made the announce's own nameless gate (S03's AC-PROF-12) double as a convenient, correct 'do nothing extra' default for every pre-existing pairingAck.test.ts test that never sets up a local-profile mock, so none of them needed touching. Once the scope was amended, the welcomeSubscription.ts fix itself was trivially small (one added argument) — the effort was entirely in correctly identifying and communicating the gap, not in resolving it.

absorbed_from.note: Reviewed the flagged scope-boundary finding (VQ-S06-007) and authorized a minimal scope amendment adding welcomeSubscription.ts, constrained to the single handlePairingAck(...) call-site argument, with explicit instructions to leave the subscription filter/dispatch order/fallthrough untouched and to add a source-scan regression test proving that. Confirms the flag-rather-than-silently-resolve approach was the right call for a genuine scope-vs-completeness tension.
